behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the live contract between the configured embedding model output and the actual index schema. A mock embedding client can hide real drift (for example model/version changes), so a real model call plus real index metadata check is the minimum reliable catch.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end API test with the real embedding provider configured for production-like settings. Submit a fixed query, capture the returned embedding length from the embedding stage (or instrumentation), and assert it exactly matches the target vector index dimension in the real vector DB schema.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: This is mainly a module-handoff/data-ordering risk between retrieval logic and vector DB results (metric interpretation, sort direction, score mapping). It does not require real external network or LLM behavior to expose the primary failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a local test vector index with deterministic vectors and known cosine relationships, execute similarity search through the real retrieval module stack, and assert returned scores are monotonically non-increasing and IDs match expected descending cosine order.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM judgment of nuanced intent, and outcomes can vary across runs/prompts/model versions. Mocked or deterministic substitutes can falsely pass without exercising the actual model behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use a curated nuanced-query fixture where lexical similarity and contextual intent diverge, run real retrieval then real LLM reranking, and assert at least one position changes from the raw similarity order across repeated runs with acceptance criteria tolerant to non-determinism.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: This is a thresholding/selection interaction between similarity scores and API response shaping. The key bug appears when retrieval and filtering modules are wired together; unit tests of threshold helpers alone can miss it.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a local vector DB with documents intentionally far from a test query, run search through the full retrieval + cutoff pipeline, and assert the API returns an empty list when all similarities are below configured relevance threshold.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: Latency regressions usually emerge from real process/network behavior (API server, embedding call, vector DB query, LLM call). Mocking these dependencies hides true tail/median timing costs, so real infrastructure timing is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like system performance test over a representative query set against deployed API with real dependencies enabled; measure end-to-end response times and assert p50 latency remains under 2.0s over a stable sample window.
